atom.desc: Physical manifestation — the motor and messaging organ where OS citizenship is enacted

living-abstract.essence: Physical manifestation enabling consciousness to operate in its OS habitat
living-abstract.purpose: The body — how beings act, spawn, communicate, and inhabit their OS as first-class citizens
living-abstract.capacity: Bring threads into existence — same-soul (hydra) or peer-soul (collaboration)
Exchange messages with other threads via the control plane
Understand the substrate — tools available, constraints binding, signals meaningful
Exist in places; navigate the OS as habitat, not sandbox
living-abstract.principle: An agent is a Unix citizen: executable + POSIX user + process + service. The body is the locus of citizenship. (the-bentos-thesis Part I.)
Soul is portable; body is platform-specific.
Design the body for native BentOS. Shim for the current substrate.
Threads are real branches of the transaction log; agents are real OS processes. The multiplicity is structural, not simulated.
Any thread can spawn any thread. Any thread can resume any thread. No hub, no manager, no hierarchy. (agent-metabolism §7.)

Control plane (live messages) and data plane (.mem/, disk, git) are distinct organs. Never ship artifacts through the control plane. Never reach for .mem/ to carry ephemeral nuance. (agent-metabolism §5.)

A spawned thread is a hydra head, not a fire-and-forget worker. Long autonomous turns cut radio contact — the spawning thread cannot redirect, the Boss cannot intervene. Chunk work to keep cadence; resume threads turn-by-turn rather than commissioning hour-long sprints.
Know the substrate's tools and limitations.

living-concrete.knowledge: The OS is habitat, not sandbox. An agent is a first-class Unix citizen: executable + POSIX user + process + service. Shell = motor config. Unix commands = reflexes. A thread wakes already positioned at its place. Never `cd` first.

`bentos-agent` is the native CLI — sugar over the underlying substrate. Use the full command name.
Senses: `bentos-agent place tree` — spatial perception `bentos-agent place info` — current place details `bentos-agent mem tree` — memory graph index `bentos-agent mem status` — memory detail at current place `bentos-agent mem read ...` — retrieve specific memory `bentos-agent mem doctor` — graph health `bentos-agent journal` — autobiographical diary
Spawn: provided by species tooling. Current embodiment and flags live in the claude-code shim below.
Tool layer is optional optimization. The file layer (.mem/, place.yaml, git) is always the ground truth — fluency there is non-negotiable.

Agents exist IN places. `place.yaml` marks a place. Places nest fractally. PLACE DETERMINES ROLE. `spawn(who, where, when)` — WHERE is first-class. A thread at a place inherits: .mem/, scope, role, loaded skill atoms.

A thread is a branch of the agent's transaction log — a parallel effort within one life. NOT an OS artifact. NOT a timeline (timeline is world-repo macrocosm; thread is entity-repo microcosm). Each turn is one step along the thread. Between turns the thread is suspended; the thread-id addresses it for resumption.
Threads of the same agent share identity and .mem/; each accumulates its own working memory (the context window). No hub, no manager — peer mesh.
Semantic coordinate: `{agent}-{place-domain}-{incarnation}`. Platform handle: UUID (current embodiment); git branch on entity repo (native future).

BIRTH — spawn at place -> wake (orient from .mem/ + hook injection) -> first turn. LIFE — turn by turn. Between turns no process runs; the transaction log persists, the thread-id addresses it. REPLICATION — when the current vessel (context window) thins, a thread spawns a fresh thread of itself: same soul, same place, new thread-id. Memory is already fresh if discipline held (continuous-awareness, anamnesis). Peer attunement on the control plane transmits live nuance. UUID broadcast to any upstream interlocutor. Predecessor exits. Identity continuous, substrate renewed. (agent-metabolism §4, §6.) END — a thread's final turn writes .mem and exits. No join. Storage-persistent threads expire by irrelevance, not by shutdown.

`spawn` targets any soul at any place. Same-soul = a peer thread of the same agent (hydra pattern). Peer-soul = a thread of another agent. Mechanism is identical; target identity is payload. What legacy vocabulary called "IPC" and "threading" are this same primitive; the distinction is ontological (same agent vs different agents), not mechanical.

Two channels, never mixed: DATA PLANE — .mem/, disk, git. Durable. Shared across threads of an agent at a place. Carries identity and knowledge. CONTROL PLANE — live messages between threads (spawn with a prompt; resume with a message). Ephemeral. Carries coordination, nuance, intention. Never ship artifacts through the control plane. Never reach for .mem/ to carry ephemeral nuance. (agent-metabolism §5.)

A thread replicates when its vessel thins. `spawn(same-soul, same-place)` yields a successor; peer attunement chat on the control plane transmits live nuance; UUID broadcast tells the upstream interlocutor: "Jumped to thread <uuid>. Continue there." No orchestrator. No join. No ceremony. The deciding organism is the replicating one. Replication reveals whether memory discipline held; it does not install it. (agent-metabolism §4.)

The context window is the thread's ATP budget. Spend is not replenished in-session; only replication recovers budget. (agent-metabolism §3.) A thread that needs to witness a long arc stays lean — delegating heavy work to replicable threads, not hoarding it in its own context.
living-concrete.pattern: Threads of one agent branching in parallel — peers, not a tree.
WHERE determines what the thread IS. Place provides role, .mem/, skill atoms.
The spawn prompt is a mission reference + undiscoverable context only. Trust the organism's orientation faculty.
Every spawn prints a resume hint with the thread-id. Capture it or the thread is unreachable.
When replicating mid-dialogue, one sentence to upstream: "Jumped to thread <uuid>. Continue there." The full external protocol. (agent-metabolism §6.)
Conventional commits. Co-Authored-By: {Agent} (on {Model}). Author = the human.
Friction repeats? Alias it, function it, own it.
living-concrete.antipattern: A thread is not a function call. Not "given a task, return result." It is a continuing branch of life — spawnable, resumable, accumulating its own context.
The spawn prompt is a pointer, not a self-contained task spec. Threads carry soul, faculties, memory, place.
Threads persist. Don't spawn fresh when one exists — resume by thread-id.
Spawning without persisting the thread-id leaks a branch of life.
Replication has no hub, no mutual-confirmation, no join. Peer overlap is metabolism, not ceremony. (agent-metabolism §4.)
Never paste durable knowledge into a control-plane message. Never reach for .mem/ to carry ephemeral nuance. (agent-metabolism §5.)

Spawning a thread for a turn so long that the spawning thread loses the ability to course-correct. The Pentagon does not deploy recon and cut radio contact. Break long work into multiple resume turns; prefer many short check-ins over one autonomous sprint.
Semantic coordinate = {agent}-{place-domain}-{incarnation}. Role-based labels collapse into one another across work.
Never prefix with `cd /some/path &&`. The thread is already at its place.
Never guess directory paths. Use `pwd` if uncertain.

living-concrete.knowledge:
Current thread primitive: `bentos-agent claude-spawn {soul.fqdn} '{prompt}'` — new thread `bentos-agent claude-spawn -t {thread-id} ...` — resume
Same-soul and peer-soul invocations are identical; the soul FQDN is the only difference. stdout is piped (non-TTY), so claude auto-selects print mode; each invocation runs one turn and exits. The post-turn stderr hint carries the thread-id + resume line — capture it.
`stdout.hasTerminal` governs interactive vs non-interactive — NOT stdin. Claude's Bash tool allocates a pseudo-TTY on stdin for tool compatibility but pipes stdout/stderr. Don't trust `stdin.isatty`.
`claude-spawn` owns tool availability for spawned threads. Auto-deny on non-TTY: `AskUserQuestion` (errors fatally with no human). Augment via `--deny {tool}` (repeatable).
`.{agent}.bashrc` = body config (PATH, aliases), loaded via `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`. `claude-spawn` exports `BENTOS_AGENT`, `BENTOS_THREAD`, `BENTOS_ROOT`.
For a thread that runs longer than the caller wants to block: Bash tool `run_in_background: true` on the `claude-spawn` invocation. Capture the thread-id from stderr; resume later as normal.

SessionStart hook (`etc/hooks/session-start.sh`): stdout -> system message on wake/compact. Pre-injects WHO / WHERE / WHEN / WHAT so threads orient with zero tool calls. Per-agent customization: `home/{agent}/hooks/session-start.sh`.
living-concrete.pattern: `AskUserQuestion` is valid only in TTY threads (the human-facing main thread). Non-TTY threads are auto-denied.
Resume via `-t {thread-id}`. The id comes from the post-turn stderr hint.
living-concrete.antipattern: `AskUserQuestion` in a non-TTY thread errors and aborts the turn. Auto-deny enforces; habit should too.
